title: Tender Silky Egyptian King Prawns Tagine
description: A lazy Sunday Egyptian dish featuring king prawns, kombu cold extract, and halal friendly ingredients, resulting in a grassy and silky texture. This dish is perfect for a relaxing weekend meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the king prawns and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until they are pink and cooked through.
Add the kombu cold extract and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the tagine for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
Traditionally, tagine is served with crusty bread or over couscous.
You can also serve it with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple green salad.
